items.title: VI Live: Atlético sloopt Real Betis aan de hand van basisdebutant Lookman
items.summary: Donderdagavond staat de laatste wedstrijd in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker op het programma. Telstar neemt het om 20.00 uur op tegen Go Ahead Eagles. Ook in het buitenland is er bekervoetbal. Blijf op de hoogte in de VI Live.

items.title: Janssen matchwinner Antwerp in eerste halve bekerfinale tegen negental Anderlecht
items.summary: Vincent Janssen mag zich matchwinner noemen in de eerste van de twee halve finales van de Croky Cup tussen Anderlecht en Antwerp FC. De Nederlandse spits kopte het enige doelpunt van de wedstrijd binnen tegen Paars-Wit , dat de wedstrijd met negen spelers uitspeelde.

items.title: Saudi Pro League behoorlijk hard tegen Cristiano Ronaldo
items.summary: De competitieorganisatie achter de Saudi Pro League heeft in een statement gereageerd op de staking van Cristiano Ronaldo, speler van Al-Nassr. In de verklaring gaat men in tegen de zienswijze van de Portugees, die nog steeds het uithangbord van de Saoedische competitie is.

items.title: Saudi league warns Ronaldo amid doubts over future
items.summary: The Saudi Pro League warns Cristiano Ronaldo "no individual" determines decisions outside of his club amid doubts over his future at Al-Nassr.

items.title: Bad Bunny on Super Bowl half-time show: 'Be proud of who you are'
items.summary: Bad Bunny has vowed to make the world happy and to make the world dance when he makes his historic appearance at the Super Bowl 60 half-time show on Sunday.

items.title: Source: Minnesota finalizing James Rodríguez deal
items.summary: Minnesota United is finalizing a deal to sign Colombia international star forward James Rodríguez, a source confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Leicester deducted 6 points for P&S rule breach
items.summary: Championship side Leicester City have been deducted six points after being found to have breached the EFL's profit and sustainability rules for the 2023/24 season.

items.title: Benzema scores debut hat trick in Al Hilal rout
items.summary: Karim Benzema made an instant impact following his contentious move to Al Hilal, scoring a hat trick on his debut for the Saudi Pro League club.

items.title: Carrick: 'No concerns' about end of United season
items.summary: Michael Carrick has insisted he's got "no concerns" that the uncertainty surrounding the manager situation at Old Trafford could derail Manchester United's end to the season.

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo warned by Saudi Pro League over transfer protest
items.summary: null

items.title: Telstar schakelt bekerhouder Go Ahead uit en schrijft clubgeschiedenis
items.summary: Telstar heeft donderdagavond voor de tweede keer in de clubhistorie de halve finale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker bereikt. Na succes in het seizoen 1991/92 is de gepromoveerde club wederom bezig aan een historisch (beker)seizoen. Donderdag werd in eigen huis bekerhouder Go Ahead Eagles uitgeschakeld.

items.title: Celtic chairman calls for unity over fan boycott plans
items.summary: Celtic interim chairman Brian Wilson calls for "unity behind the team" amid plans from some fans to boycott Saturday's Scottish Cup match against Dundee.

items.title: Wanneer is de loting van AZ in de Youth League?
items.summary: De Nederlandse inbreng in de UEFA Youth League is teruggebracht tot één club. AZ is na de play-offs de enige overgebleven vertegenwoordiger, nadat PSV en Ajax werden uitgeschakeld. Vrijdag 6 februari wordt in het Zwitserse Nyon geloot voor het vervolg van het toernooi.

items.title: Does spending £60m on a young player pay off in the Premier League?
items.summary: Liverpool paid £60m for the 20-year-old defender Jérémy Jacquet. Previous deals in the league suggest it is a risk By WhoScored As Premier League teams get younger and transfer fees get bigger, we are seeing players with little experience commanding huge prices. Jérémy Jacquet’s £60m move to Liverpool makes him the fourth most expensive player aged 20 or younger in Premier League history. Spending so much money for a defender with just 36 appearances in France’s top flight looks like a risk, but how have players Jacquet’s age fared after big moves? Continue reading...

items.title: 'Makes me sick' - how Tom Brady created a Super Bowl storm
items.summary: Tom Brady may not be playing in the Super Bowl, but he remains a topic of discussion as the New England Patriots prepare to face the Seattle Seahawks this Sunday.

items.title: Seahawks star expected to play at Super Bowl after injury scare
items.summary: The Seattle Seahawks are expected to have Nick Emmanwori available for Sunday's Super Bowl despite a late injury scare for the star defender.

items.title: Draper makes winning return from injury
items.summary: British star Jack Draper has made a winning return after being sidelined for a lengthy spell by injury.

items.title: Frank says Romero ‘dealt with internally’ for latest volley at Spurs owners
items.summary: Manager refuses to criticise captain for outburst ‘Too much attention’ given to social media post Thomas Frank has refused to criticise Cristian Romero following his social media attack on Tottenham’s owners, saying the matter has been “dealt with internally”. The Tottenham manager made his ambivalence about social media clear – “something I do know is that we all give it too much attention” – but stopped short of condemning his captain’s behaviour after last weekend’s draw with Manchester City, despite it being the second such outburst in as many months. Continue reading...

items.title: Leicester docked six points for financial breaches
items.summary: Leicester City are docked six points by the English Football League for breaking profit and sustainability rules.

items.title: Leicester City in relegation danger after six-point deduction for financial rules breach
items.summary: Leicester breached PSR rules for period ending 2023-24 Club outside the relegation zone on goal difference Leicester have been deducted six points after being found in breach of the Premier League’s financial rules. The punishment, determined by an independent disciplinary commission, leaves them outside the Championship relegation zone only on goal difference. A hearing took place in November after Leicester were alleged to have breached profitability and sustainability rules (PSR) for the three-season period ending with 2023-24. There were also two further charges against the club for failing to cooperate and failing to submit their financial accounts on time. Continue reading...
